fuzzy_collisions.reason: tokens 'entity' + 'id' match an existing column on the existing audit_logs table (ts_line 671). The proposed admin_audit_logs table appears to be a near-duplicate of audit_logs with extra dual-approval/tenant fields; reviewer must decide whether to (a) extend audit_logs with tenant_id/reason_code/requested_by/approved_by/exec_status/payload columns, or (b) keep admin_audit_logs as a separate canonical privileged-action log distinct from the existing generic audit_logs.
Single-token 'action' column already exists on the closely-named audit_logs table (ts_line 671); reinforces the table-level synonym concern above.

notes: Existing audit_logs table at ts_line 671 already records id, entity_type, entity_id, action, performed_by, old_data, new_data, created_at, changes, timestamp. The proposed admin_audit_logs duplicates the entity_id+action+timestamp shape but adds tenant_id, reason_code, requested_by, approved_by, exec_status, payload. The BRD's RBI Digital Lending Directions 2025 compliance need is real — but the spec needs a human to choose between extending the existing audit_logs table or keeping a parallel admin_audit_logs.
If the team chooses to extend audit_logs, several downstream units (E-068, E-070, E-072) that write/reuse 'admin_audit_logs' must be re-targeted.
No cross-doc gap (G-01..G-10) directly impacts admin_audit_logs.
